params.name: Kanner's Report
params.description:
On Jan 14, 1943 AD, Dr. Kenna discovered something unusual in his patient's medical records.
Kenna's Medical Report is the world's first medical report on autism, in which Kenna analyzed in detail the 11 patients he was responsible for, and summarized the following characteristics: severe lack of emotional contact with others, repetitive ritual behaviors, high levels of vision, and alert in some ways, which are roughly consistent with the characteristics of today's autism.
